description:
Goal: the docs should describe the corrected Compiler2 engine after activation input becomes a joined fact, runtime bootstrap becomes lazy, and Compiler2 owns one local interned type world instead of borrowing old-world type handles and abstractions. Signal: northstar.html, .agent/docs/telemetry.md, the ignored quicksort JSONL dump harness, and the trace-summary helper all agree with the code.

Strategy: update docs only after the code has settled, then keep the deep trace and summary probe as durable guideposts for backend work.

Change:
- Update northstar.html with activation-input fact joins, Recursive/DispatchMask facts, value-based fact aggregation, read-only semantic closure, Compiler2-owned local `Types`/`Ty`, exact within-run revision/change tracking, and lazy runtime bootstrap.
- Update .agent/docs/telemetry.md with the Compiler2 rule: emit raw ids/facts/structures only, emit state-derived events only when state actually changes, and treat local type ids as compiler-owned world facts.
- Document the ignored quicksort JSONL dump test and the quicksort trace-summary assertions.

Why:
- Backend work should resume against docs that match the settled engine rather than the pre-type-world cleanup shape.

Checks:
- Docs match the code, summary assertions, and deep JSONL trace.
- Harness paths and rerun commands are captured.
title: Compiler2 docs: publish the corrected fixpoint and telemetry contract
